subject: Re: wrpCoordinates after M refine
body:
Hi Mingyu,

If the resolution got worse it's because you're overfitting - your use of
temporally resolved particle poses is a good sign that you should be taking
a bit more care when using M. M is a refinement engine capable of refining
lots of parameters and getting the best results out of your data, your data
may not be strong enough to support refinement of the parameters you are
refining.

In your position I would go back to the state of your processing directory
prior to running M, set up the species with a single temporal pose and
start by refining only 1x1 image warp and particle poses. Only once this
stops improving would I start adding in more parameters.

Cheers,

Alister

On Sat, 19 Oct 2024 at 10:04, Mingyu Jung <jmg0910@snu.ac.kr> wrote:

> Hi Alister,
>
> Thank you for your quick reply!
>
> After acquiring weird fsc, I tried to re-extract my particles using same
> pixel size. And I recognized my particles were disappeared.
> So I re-import my raw data and re-processed the tomostar file. Then I
> could re-extract particles but acquired poor resolution. (Before the
> refining issue: 7.5A -> re-process tomostar and re-extract 9.5A)
> Do you know the causes of these weird results? I guessed when I refined in
> M, tilt-series alignment or ctf would be changed...
>
> Best,
> Mingyu
>
> 2024년 10월 18일 금요일 오전 12시 2분 1초 UTC+9에 alist...@gmail.com님이 작성:
>
>> Hi Mingyu,
>>
>> I’m not sure about the outputs and what version you’re using but at least
>> internally Warp/M uses positions relative to the center of the tomogram in
>> angstroms - I think this is likely what’s in the output but haven’t checked.
>>
>> It’s unlikely that you have enough signal for temporally resolved poses
>> to be useful unless you’re at 3A or so - if that’s not the case you are
>> likely overfitting these parameters
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>
>> On Oct 17, 2024, at 05:20, Mingyu Jung <jmg...@snu.ac.kr> wrote:
>>
>> ﻿Hi all,
>>
>>
>>
>> I have a question about wrpCoordinates value in particles.star file in
>> species folder after M refinement.
>>
>> data_
>>
>> loop_
>> _wrpCoordinateX1 #1
>> _wrpCoordinateY1 #2
>> _wrpCoordinateZ1 #3
>> _wrpCoordinateX2 #4
>> _wrpCoordinateY2 #5
>> _wrpCoordinateZ2 #6
>> _wrpCoordinateX3 #7
>> _wrpCoordinateY3 #8
>> _wrpCoordinateZ3 #9

>> _wrpAngleRot2 #13

>> _wrpAngleRot3 #16

>> 4359.67236  4262.32910  1007.64624  4359.67236  4262.32910  1007.64624
>>  4359.67236  4262.32910  1007.64624   77.43541  63.04351    68.49342
>> 77.43541  63.04351    68.49342   77.43541  63.04351    68.49342  1
>> Position_123_3.tomostar  tczm1sxAEOHlvCpqjkGwUyb06vg
>>
>> some of my particle coordinates are higher than 4096 which is physically
>> not acceptable. I wondered whether the wrpcoordinates are actual pixel
>> values or not. In addition, I set unbinned tomogram dimension
>> 3712x3712x2000.
>>
>> Thank you!
>>
>> Best,
>> Mingyu
>>
